SELF TAUGHT COMPLIANCE
Scaled my own fitness brand to multi-millions spending 10-15k a day.
Managed compliance for 5000 ads and 99.8% compliant ads vs
disapprovals.
Now my agency manages millions per month in spend, working with
big clients + multiple reps.
Lots of clients in the health, financial, biz ops, supplement + ecomm
space.
Helping create over 200 million $ a year in revenue via our clients,
alot of which is directly via FB cold traffic.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 18 --

GENERAL COMPLIANCE RULES
When making any sort of claims, add disclaimer to the bottom of the ad.
If making claim, we’ve found it best to make broader claims
Speak in FIRST or THIRD person, but avoid the use of “you”
Avoid income related terms like “Figures or “$” and shorten. For example,
6 figs, 5K, etc.
Try not to ‘call people out’ or ‘target their issues’
Submit manual reviews if compliant, often they get approved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 18 --

WEBSITE COMPLIANCE
Facebook likes real websites - the URL and page matters.
Add nav menu, and build them out with strong copy. For
example, an about section that ‘sells’, a blog that is really an
advertorial, etc.
Ensure you’ve got a footer with terms, contract info, privacy
policy etc.
Avoid pop ups or anything that ‘reduces the users ability to
navigate’
Place your best conv boosting parts / testimonials / non
compliant parts on the checkout page.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 18 --

VIDEO ADS + COMPLIANCE
We manually add captions vs FB adding them, this tends to help
with approvals.
We rolled out slideshow videos for all our clients that cover the
key points in a story board. In 80% of cases, it’s beating static
copy variations and easier for compliance.
Perfect to leverage and show testimonials that tend to be easier
for compliance than statements from you.
Example 1 (3.2ROAS) / Example 2 (3.0 ROAS) / Example 3 (50%
Reduction in Web Reg cost)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 18 --

SALES AD COMPLIANCE
Avoid writing in second person as much as possible
When making claims and stating facts, use reference numbers linking
to 3rd-party sources
Ad images of claims instead of copy to bypass compliance
Focus on positive, vs negative.
Find key words for your space that pass compliance, for example
instead of ‘fat loss’ we use ‘physique transformation’
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 18 --

FINANCIAL FB COMPLIANCE EXAMPLE
● 	Currently 2.5 - 3x ROAS to cold.
● 	First person -- positioned as a testimonial vs. ‘you can
make $XXX by doing this’
● 	Promoted as a training (education) vs. buying a program
(sale)
● 	Narrative style vs. promotional language
● 	Bold, potentially controversial statement.
● 	Giving average no. of earnings based on his earnings, vs
telling people they can make that amount.
● 	Lifestyle shot vs. stock photo makes it relatable
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 18 --

BIZ OP COACHING
● 	4-5x ROAS to mainly cold.
● 	Bold and direct ‘YES’ statement calling out prospect’s
pain point
● 	Personal story/hero’s journey angle
● 	High-contrast and engaging video
● 	First person -- positioned as a testimonial vs. ‘you can
make $XXX by doing this’
● 	No direct sale, CTA is only asking to show interest to
receive a message (via Messenger bot)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 18 --

3 SECRETS 	- 	EXAMPLE 2
● 	Removed dollar signs without losing
context
● 	Used ‘pm’ to indicate ‘per month’
● 	No big claims being made, focused
more on giving away free education
● 	No attempt to make the sale, went to
webinar.
● 	Focused on case study vs promise.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 18 --

DIABETES / HEALTH
● 	$40 CPA to cold
● 	No scientific claims made in the copy,
only alluding to science-backed
methods
● 	Positioned as an article to create
intrigue
● 	Only 2 lines of copy, easy to
understand in seconds and get the
click.
● 	Focused on MANAGE not CURE
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 18 --

WEIGHT LOSS / SUPPS
● 	Avg acc is 3k spend at 66% of CPA
goal per day.
● 	Bullet points makes it easy to
read/digest. Eye catching graphic.
● 	Attractive offer: 3 free jars
● 	No actual claims made about ‘fixing’
health problems
● 	Qualifying question in the beginning
turns this into less aggressive sales
copy
● 	Avoid trigger words.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 18 --

METABOLISM 	- 	EXAMPLE 2
● 	1, 2, 3 sections help break up copy
for easy reading
● 	Talk about metabolism vs weight
loss.
● 	Talk about benefits of boosting
metabolism, vs the benefits of the
product.
● 	For education based, vs claim
based.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 18 --

HEALTH / SUPPLEMENT EXAMPLE
● 	Over 1 million $ spent at 3.5x
ROAS to cold.
● 	Over 2000 ads, 98% compliance
rating.
● 	Shock effect and eye catching
images.
● 	Lots of educational (blog post) vs.
sale
● 	Short and direct copy without an
offer can convert better
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 18 --

IF ALL ELSE FAILS… BACK-UP
COMPLIANCE GUIDELINES
- 	Set up a 2nd ad acc with pixel all over site, then share
that across. That way, you never lose pixel data.
- 	Have back up accounts ready and verified.
- 	When building funnels, we tend to make 1 FB
compliant one and one more aggressive.
- 	Get a lot of credit cards, haha!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
